commit.message:
Remove JS text truncation in table formatters, let CSS line-clamp handle it

Removed the 60-char details truncation and 24-char secret truncation from
JS formatters — CSS 3-line clamp handles visual truncation, and the removed
JS truncation was preventing tooltip overflow detection from triggering.
Also improved tooltip handler with dual detection: JS truncation check
(tip length vs visible text) and CSS clamp check (unset clamp to measure
true scrollHeight).

Fix tooltip overflow detection for line-clamped cells

Previous scrollHeight check didn't work with -webkit-line-clamp.
Now temporarily unsets the clamp to measure true content height,
then compares against clamped height to decide if tooltip is needed.

Fix graph ordering: WEB01 after DC01, access→host edges

assignment places pivot destinations after the source host. Added
unconditional access→host edges so the chain flows correctly:
cred → access → host → [pivot] → next host.

commit.message:
Add via_access_id provenance tracking to kill-chain graph

Schema v3→v4 migration adds via_access_id FK on credentials and vulns
tables, linking discoveries to the access session that found them.
State-server add_credential/add_vuln accept the new parameter.
State-viewer graph uses via_access_id for explicit edge inference
(access→vuln, access→cred) with heuristic fallback for NULL values.
Access nodes are pre-created before vuln/cred processing so references
resolve correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: red-run
repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

commit.message:
Fix provided-cred chain ordering and reduce edge crossings

Provided-cred chain detection was fragile — parsed node labels to
find hosts. Now tracks hostsViaProvidedCred directly during access
node creation when matching against a provided credential. This
ensures attacker->cred->access->host->downstream ordering instead
of cred appearing as a sibling of the host.

Also broadened cred->access matching to check access.method for
the credential username (catches cases where username formats
differ between cred and access records).

Added barycenter heuristic for node ordering within layers (3
passes). Nodes in each layer are sorted by the average Y position
of their parent nodes, which reduces edge crossings — e.g., the
DC->gMSA_ADCS edge no longer has to route through credential boxes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix kill-chain ordering: provided creds flow attacker->cred->access->host

When a credential is provided (source matches "pentest", "provided",
etc.), the chain now flows: Attacker -> cred -> access -> host ->
downstream findings. Previously the host got a direct edge from
Attacker, making the cred appear as a sibling instead of an ancestor.

Changes:
- Skip attacker->host edge for hosts reachable via provided-cred chain
- Add access->host bridge edges so downstream nodes (vulns, pivots,
  blocked) that connect from the host node stay in the chain

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: red-run
repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

commit.message:
Prune dead-end vulns from graph, link provided creds from Attacker

Graph now only shows vulns that chain into something downstream
(credential, access). Dead-end findings that didn't lead anywhere
are pruned from the graph but remain in the Vulns table.

Credentials whose source matches "provided", "scope", "pentest",
"given", "initial", or "pre-engagement" now link from the Attacker
node instead of from a host — reflecting that they were supplied
as part of scope, not discovered through exploitation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

commit.message:
Render pivots as labeled edges instead of nodes

Pivots are relationships, not entities — they now render as styled
edges between source access/host and destination host. Edge label
shows method + status (e.g. "sshuttle", "chisel (identified)").
Exploited pivots are solid green, identified are dashed yellow,
blocked are dotted red. Removes the uninformative column of
identical "pivot" boxes.

Edge legend labels updated: Exploited/Identified/Blocked.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Improve kill-chain graph: subtitles, legend, manual refresh

Graph nodes now show two lines — label + contextual subtitle:
- Hosts: IP + role/OS
- Vulns: title + severity (color-coded by severity level)
- Credentials: user + secret_type (cracked)
- Access: user@host + type|privilege
- Pivots: method + source->destination (instead of generic "pivot")
- Blocked: technique + truncated reason

Adds a legend bar below the graph showing node shapes/colors and
edge styles (confirmed/pending/blocked).

SSE auto-refresh now only updates cards + tables. Graph only redraws
on explicit Refresh button click to prevent disruptive reloads.

commit.message:
Add state-viewer web dashboard with kill-chain graph

Read-only browser dashboard for engagement/state.db served at localhost:8099.
Stdlib-only Python HTTP server with inline HTML/CSS/JS — no dependencies.

Features:
- Kill-chain SVG attack graph (hosts, vulns, creds, access, pivots, blocked)
- State tables with sorting, filtering, collapsible sections
- SSE live updates (events every 2s, full refresh every 10s)
- Token-based auth for remote access (generate-token.sh binds 0.0.0.0)
- HMAC-signed session cookies, Bearer header support for API clients
- Graceful degradation when DB doesn't exist yet

Closes #64

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: correct field name mismatches between API and CLI, fix seed logic

- CLI timeline: read event.emotionalState (not emotionalContext) and
  event.content (not metadata) to match EpisodicEvent schema
- CLI mysteries: use API's cluesFound/totalClues/clues (string[])
  instead of treating clues as objects with .revealed/.text
- CLI secrets: read secret.type/hint (API shape) not secretType/hintText
  (Prisma model field names)
- RoutineService: use actual detected period for companion routine type
  instead of hardcoded 'evening_companion'
- MysteryService: use word-boundary regex for pronoun replacement in
  seed secrets to prevent substring corruption (theirs/their/them/they)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Claude/explore curtis brag codebase fb3 gz (#149)

* Add authenticated cluster control panel dashboard

Create /cluster/dashboard page with password-protected controls for
managing the phone cluster: quick actions (wake/sleep/start/stop/restart/
reboot/update), mining controls with level slider, display mode switching,
brightness control, SSH command execution with output polling, URL browsing,
screenshot capture/viewing, and command queue/history monitoring. Uses
existing cluster-control.js API with sessionStorage auth. Move cluster.astro
to cluster/index.astro for proper subdirectory routing.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix security: add auth to flush-queue and auth-check endpoint

- Add password auth to flush-queue GET endpoint (was unauthenticated)
- Add dedicated auth-check endpoint so login doesn't fire a real debug
  command to node1
- Update dashboard to use auth-check for login and pass password to
  flush-queue

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Remove old static dashboard.html that was overriding Astro page

public/cluster/dashboard.html (6090-line standalone page) was being
served by Netlify instead of the Astro-built dashboard at
src/pages/cluster/dashboard.astro. The static file took priority,
so the new control panel with auth was never being served.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Remove stale redirects pointing /cluster/dashboard to deleted HTML file

netlify.toml had explicit redirects from /cluster/dashboard to
/cluster/dashboard.html which no longer exists (deleted in previous
commit). These redirects prevented Astro's dashboard/index.html from
being served. Removing them lets Netlify serve the Astro-built page.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix poll script: add PC nodes and proper target group expansion

- Add PC nodes (neo, vikixii, nexus-prime, steamdeck) to cluster-nodes.conf
  with "pc" role, using Tailscale hostnames for SSH resolution
- Add PC_NODES group and resolve_target_nodes() helper for consistent
  target expansion across all commands
- "all" now targets phones+PCs for general commands (start/stop/restart/
  mining/reboot/ssh), phones only for phone-specific commands
- "phones" targets phone nodes only, "pcs" targets PC nodes only
- Phone-specific commands (wake/sleep/display-mode/browse/brightness/
  screenshot) return "skipped" when targeted at "pcs"
- control-plane nodes now correctly included in PHONE_NODES

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix mining commands: scope to phones only and capture error output

- mining-start/stop/level now use "phones" scope instead of "all" —
  PCs don't have xmrig so targeting them caused false failures
- mining-start now captures per-node stdout and reports it back via
  the output field, so the dashboard shows WHY nodes failed (e.g.
  "xmrig not installed", service status, etc.)
- Fixed mining-start command to print errors to stdout instead of
  stderr so run_on_node captures them properly

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix mining-start: auto-create xmrig systemd service if missing

The phones run systemd but had no xmrig.service file, causing
mining-start to fail with "Unit xmrig.service could not be found".
Now mining-start auto-creates the service file, reloads systemd,
and ensures the xmrig binary symlink exists before starting.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix mining-start: auto-create xmrig config.json if missing

xmrig was exiting with status=2 because /etc/xmrig/config.json
didn't exist on the phones. Now mining-start auto-creates both
the config (with pool/wallet/worker settings) and the systemd
service file per-node before starting.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix mining-start: use printf instead of nested heredocs

The previous approach used nested heredocs in a function inside a
case block, which crashed BusyBox ash on the phones after update.
Simplified to a single-quoted variable with printf for both config
and service file creation. Uses hostname for worker name.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix bad merge: remove stale function code from mining-start

GitHub merge produced a frankenstein with old function-based code
(MSCRIPT_BOT, build_mining_start_cmd) mixed into the printf version.
This caused a syntax error on line 487 crashing the poller on startup.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Move MINING_START_CMD to top of script for BusyBox ash compat

BusyBox ash on the phones fails to parse multi-line single-quoted
strings containing if/else/fi inside case blocks. Moving the command
to the top level as a single-line string fixes the parse error at
line 487 that was crashing the poller on startup.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix pandas compatibility and correct Hachemeister reference values

- Remove include_groups=False in groupby.apply() -- not supported in pandas
  versions before 2.2. Use column pre-selection instead, which works from
  pandas 1.3 onwards.
- Correct Hachemeister dataset reference values: the research document had
  fabricated actuar output. Computed values directly from the B-S estimators:
  mu=1832.816, v=136793600.7, a=100301.9, k=1363.818.
- Fix test_bottom_premiums_equals_premiums_at_sector: premiums_ returns
  hierarchically blended values; premiums_at("sector") returns the
  sector-level-only estimates. They share an index, not identical values.
- All 41 tests pass on Databricks serverless compute (pandas 1.x environment).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: 实现核心数据统计和分享解锁功能

## 核心功能

### 1. 4个核心数据指标
- ✅ 总练习次数
- ✅ 连续练习天数
- ✅ 最常练的场景Top3
- ✅ 流失预警（3天未练习提醒）

### 2. 分享解锁机制
- ✅ 每天3次免费练习（不限场景）
- ✅ 超过3次后可分享解锁
- ✅ 每天可分享3次，每次解锁1次练习机会
- ✅ 好友点进分享链接也可获得1次练习机会
- ✅ 当天有效，过期自动清除

## 数据库变更

### 新增表结构
- users表新增字段：daily_free_count, daily_free_date, daily_share_count, daily_share_date
- share_records表：记录用户分享行为
- unlock_records表：记录用户解锁机会

### 优化练习记录
- 删除unique约束，支持同一天多次练习记录
- 每次练习都插入新记录，准确统计

## API接口

### 统计接口
- GET /api/scenarios/core-statistics - 获取4个核心指标

### 分享解锁接口
- GET /api/share/check-status - 检查练习状态
- POST /api/share/record - 记录分享并解锁
- POST /api/share/handle-invite - 处理好友进入
- POST /api/share/consume - 消耗练习次数

## 前端变更

### 个人中心
- 新增统计卡片展示：总次数、连续天数、Top3场景
- 集成后端统计API
- 实现流失预警提醒

### 场景列表
- 练习前检查次数
- 次数用完弹出分享引导
- 引导分享或开通会员

commit.message:
Make all JSON writes atomic to prevent file corruption (#18)

Replace direct open('file.json', 'w') + json.dump with atomic
write-to-tmp-then-rename pattern in all inline Python blocks:
- agent-runner.sh: claim_task(), mark_task(), release_clone_lock()
- agent-pool: auto_migrate()
- finish-task.sh (installed copy, not tracked in repo)

os.rename() is atomic on POSIX when src/dest are on the same
filesystem. The .tmp file is always in the same directory as
the target, ensuring atomicity. This prevents 0-byte corruption
when a process is interrupted between open('w') and write.

The CLI's write_pool/write_projects/write_tasks helpers already
used this pattern via cat > tmp && mv.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
